subject: Changes to fix db connection issues in xacml-pdp
commit_message:
Changes to fix db connection issues in xacml-pdp

Added code to copy the oom xacml.properties file to the proper directory
Made change based on Jorge's comments

Change-Id: I0c46a01ca4407fa4d18391ca9ad81d37a0f46969
Issue-ID: POLICY-1760
Signed-off-by: Michael Mokry <michael.mokry@att.com>

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

awaiting fix per Pam's last comment

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

The origin source file in the cp command should be ..

XACML_FILE="/opt/app/policy/pdpx/etc/mounted/xacml.properties"

As it would be mounted there.  But to be more generic I think what you what should be done is to pass a new argument to the script, let’s say that you have 3 fixed arguments, something similar to this:

if [[  -z $3 ]]; then
	XACML_FILE =$3
else
	XACML_FILE="${POLICY_PDPX_HOME}/etc/xacml.properties"
Fi

If [[ -f ${XACML_FILE} ]]; then
    cp -f “${XACML_FILE}”  ${POLICY_PDPX_HOME}”/apps/guard/
fi

In your deployment.yaml in oom, you need to modify the “container” section the array with the arguments you pass to
Include a third one.   The contents would be 

args: ["/opt/app/policy/pdpx/etc/mounted/config.json","/opt/app/policy/pdpx/etc/mounted/topic.properties","/opt/app/policy/pdpx/etc/mounted/xacml.properties"]


That’s how all should be wired I think, better than to make an assumption where the xacml.properties will be mounted.

The short route would be just to do this change:

XACML_FILE="/opt/app/policy/pdpx/etc/mounted/xacml.properties"

I think that should work but you're making assumptions in the image about the mounted location which is a shortcoming.

I don't think that will work - the applications are given a path off the "apps" directory to look for their respective xacml.properties.

Patch Set 3:

I guess I am not sure about the destination location where to place it, you guys know better, once the container is running.  The master copy as I understand it from Mike is placed outside of the container and from outside ../etc/mounted dir (same as the config.json) is the name of the directory that is made available to the container on the helm charts.

Patch Set 3:

> I guess I am not sure about the destination location where to place
 > it, you guys know better, once the container is running.  The
 > master copy as I understand it from Mike is placed outside of the
 > container and from outside ../etc/mounted dir (same as the
 > config.json) is the name of the directory that is made available to
 > the container on the helm charts.

When we had the discussion in Rocketchat I posted the tree layout that is contained in the tarball:
policy/xacml-pdp/packages/policy-xacmlpdp-tarball/src/main/resources/apps.
The config JSON file has an entry that points to the apps directory.
Mike knows this because he put the package together. So we have to determine how to replicate the apps directory for Helm charts and then modify ONLY the xacml.properties under the apps/guard directory.

Patch Set 3:

I think the only way copying the file works then is if I make a 3rd optional commandline argument for the xacml.properties destination and modify the java code to overwrite the default xacml.properties in app/guard during startup if the option is used otherwise go with default property file.

Or similar to policy/engine, I can create a .conf file in OOM that sets the variable for the mariadb url and use that in the existing xacml.properties file something like this..

javax.persistence.jdbc.url=${{JDBC_URL}}

This would allow it to be configurable from OOM but I will need some help here as I do not know how to implement this 100%.. looking at the policy/engine deployment.yaml it seems a bit confusing.

Patch Set 3:

This should work fine I believe Mike.   For the sake of time, I suggest adopting this simplified version so to have it available for release tomorrow.   The code will only be executed in an OOM installation:
-----
if [[ -f ${POLICY_HOME}/etc/mounted/xacml.properties ]]; then
    cp -f “${POLICY_HOME}"/etc/mounted/xacml.properties  "${POLICY_HOME}"/apps/guard/
fi
-----

A couple notes you can use POLICY_HOME or POLICY_PDPX_HOME they should point to the same.   The base Dockerfile uses POLICY_HOME so it is available and should work.

“${POLICY_HOME}/etc/mounted/xacml.properties” is fixed and will only appear in an oom installation, so the copy will only apply in those cases and that's what you want.

Looking at the layout structure of pdp-x that Pam referred to and in the container, the guard location is ${POLICY_HOME}”/apps/guard/ and you're providing this external xacml.properties.

I think that's the path of least resistence so we can release tomorrow.   After that you could add any enhancements you may want to it.

Patch Set 3:

Please follow Jorge's suggestion, I do not want the mess that was made in policy/engine.
